signals.title: USD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Yield Dis
signals.text: JPY 1W avg +1.08% (n=7) outperforms USD 1W avg -0.87% (n=7) with a G8 spread of 1.95%; the 196bp US-JGB yield gap runs against JPY momentum — crowded short -88.5k sets up squeeze risk. The next BoJ meeting is the catalyst for resolving the yield gap. Trump's tariff escalation on European autos may further impact USD direction.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Tariff Escalation and ECB Hold
signals.text: EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1723; Trump tariff escalation on EU autos sets up downside pressure toward 1.16 — EUR COT short -18.3k reinforces the bias. The ECB at 2.00% with next ECB meeting may further influence EUR/USD direction. Geopolitical risk premium is secondary to the 175bp USD-EUR carry advantage.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Hike Bias and Carry Advant
signals.text: AUD COT LF long +48.3k (74% long) creates crowded-long unwind risk — AUD/USD closed 0.7208 Friday; RBA at 4.10% with Hold bias (0% priced) sustains the bid but any disappointment triggers the unwind. The 35bp AUD-USD carry advantage supports the AUD bid.

signals.title: GBP/USD — BoE Hold and Cross-Asset Corre
signals.text: GBP/USD closed Friday at 1.3575; the BoE at 3.75% with next BoE meeting is likely to limit GBP/USD upside. The positive correlation between GBP/USD and FTSE 100, currently at +0.325, supports the pair's direction.

signals.title: NZD/USD — RBNZ Hike Bias and Retail Sent
signals.text: NZD/USD closed Friday at 0.5898; RBNZ hike bias (next meeting on 27 May) is likely to support NZD/USD. However, retail sentiment shows 61% of traders are short, which is likely to indicate a contrarian reversal risk.
